code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test22-NOV-2019Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, with ammoniacal odor, but no nitrites or leukocytes, making infection less likely. Presence of glucose, bilirubin, and ketones in urine may indicate metabolic disturbances. Recommend further nephrological evaluation for possible renal insufficiency and assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic disease.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
